metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Sources: official Stonemaier rulebook, BGG FAQ, r/boardgames strategy threads. BGG rank: 650, rating: 7.1, weight: 3.0.

key_rulings.text: The first player to place all 10 of their authority tokens wins immediately.

When you roll dice, sum the values of ALL your available (off-board) workers plus your knowledge track position. If the total is 16 or higher, you lose the available worker with the highest knowledge value.
Commodities (water, energy, food, bliss) are generated directly by worker placement. Resources (gold, stone, clay) require commodities to produce via the resource markets.
Players who contribute to building a market each place 1 authority token on it. Players who did NOT contribute suffer that market's ongoing penalty.

Each faction has an allegiance track with 4 tiers. Tier 1: +1 commodity at that faction's area. Tier 2: tunnels grant both options. Tier 3: all hidden recruits of that faction activate. Tier 4: place 1 authority star on each active recruit of that faction.
Your position on the morale track determines your hand size for artifact cards. Higher morale = more cards in hand.
The knowledge track determines your worker desertion threshold. If your workers' total knowledge exceeds this number when retrieved, you lose a worker.
When a tunnel is completed (all spaces filled by workers), each player with a worker in that tunnel places 1 authority token. All workers are then returned to their owners.
If you place a worker on a space already occupied by another player's worker, you bump their worker back to them. If you place on your own worker, you get the benefit of both dice values.
Pay 3 Energy to gain a new worker (lose 2 morale) or pay 3 Water to gain a new worker (gain 2 morale). Start with 2 workers, max 4.
If at the start of your turn you have 2+ available workers showing the same number, you may place all matching workers on that turn, resolving each individually.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Here's everything you'll find in the box. Take a moment to familiarize yourself with each piece before you start setting up.

#### Board
You'll see **1 large game board** showing a dystopian world split into 4 faction territories: Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander, and Icarite. Each territory has its own commodity generation spaces, resource markets, tunnels, and construction sites. Right in the center, you'll find the knowledge and morale tracks that every player shares.

#### Worker Dice
You get **24 custom dice** (4 per player in 6 colors) — and here's the thing, these dice ARE your workers. Each face shows a knowledge level from 1 to 6. When you place a worker, the number showing on that die is that worker's knowledge — and it matters a lot.

#### Cards
- **48 recruit cards** — each one belongs to one of the 4 factions and gives you a special ability. You'll start with 2 recruits: 1 face-up (active right away) and 1 face-down (it activates later when you earn that faction's allegiance).
- **36 artifact cards** — you'll collect these from certain board spaces and turn them in as sets to place authority tokens.

#### Tokens & Markers
- **60 authority tokens** (10 per player) — these are star-shaped pieces in your player color. Place all 10 to win the game.
- **Commodity tokens** — Water (blue drops), Energy (yellow lightning), Food (brown), Bliss (purple clouds). You generate these by placing your workers on commodity spaces.
- **Resource tokens** — Gold (yellow bars), Stone (gray blocks), Clay (red bricks). You produce these at resource markets by spending your commodities.
- **6 knowledge markers** and **6 morale markers** — you get one of each, and they sit on their respective tracks throughout the game.

#### Markets & Structures
- **6 market tiles** — these are double-sided and go on the construction sites. When you help build one, you place an authority token on it. Anyone who didn't help suffers that market's ongoing penalty.
- **Ethical dilemma cards** — one-time-use choices you can trigger during play.

#### Other
- **1 progress token** for tracking allegiance
- **1 rulebook** (12 pages)
- **GameTrayz insert**

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
Euphoria is a **worker-placement** game where your workers are dice. You're building a dystopian city, racing to place all **10 authority tokens** before anyone else. Here's the twist: your workers have **knowledge levels** (the numbers on the dice). If they get too smart, they desert — you lose them. You need to keep your workers dumb enough to stay while using them effectively.

#### The 4 Factions
Before you set up, get to know who's who in this dystopia:
- **Euphorians** — city dwellers who generate **energy** (yellow lightning). They power the electric grid.
- **Subterrans** — underground miners who generate **water** (blue drops). They tunnel beneath the city.
- **Wastelanders** — scrapyard survivors who generate **food** (brown). They scavenge the ruins.
- **Icarites** — cloud-city elites who generate **bliss** (purple clouds). They float above it all in airships.

Each faction controls its own territory on the board with unique commodity spaces and mechanics.

#### Setting Up
1. **Place the board** in the center of the table. Stock commodity and resource tokens in their supply areas.
2. **Grab your starting pieces:** 2 worker dice in your color, 10 authority tokens, 1 knowledge marker, and 1 morale marker.
3. **Roll your 2 starting dice.** The numbers showing are your workers' starting knowledge levels. Add them together and place your knowledge marker at that total on the knowledge track.
4. **Deal 4 recruit cards** to each player. Choose 2 to keep (return the others to the box unseen). Place 1 recruit **face-up** (active immediately) and 1 **face-down** (this one activates later when you earn that faction's allegiance).
5. **Draw artifact cards** equal to your morale track hand size.
6. **Place market tiles** randomly on the construction sites.
7. **Choose a starting player.**

#### Checkpoint
Before your first turn, make sure everyone has: 2 worker dice (rolled, numbers visible), 10 authority tokens in their color, markers on both the knowledge and morale tracks, 1 face-up recruit, 1 face-down recruit, and artifact cards in hand equal to your morale-based hand size. On the board, you should see commodity areas stocked, construction sites with market tiles, and resource markets ready to go.

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
On your turn, you do exactly **one** of two things:

#### Option A: Place a Worker
Pick up 1 of your available worker dice and place it on an action space on the board. You immediately resolve that space's effect.

**Bumping:** If you place on a bumpable space (like a commodity area) where an opponent's worker already sits, you **bump** their worker back to them — they have to roll it and perform a knowledge check. You still get the space's benefit. This is one of the most interactive parts of the game.

**Doubles rule:** At the start of your turn, check your available workers. If 2 or more show the **same number**, you can place all matching workers on that turn, resolving each one individually. This is a massive tempo swing — you're effectively taking multiple actions in one turn.

Now let's look at where you can actually place your workers:
- **Commodity areas** (Generator, Aquifer, Farm, Cloud Mine) — you gain commodities here. The benefit scales with the **total knowledge of all workers** on that space: low total = 1 commodity + allegiance advance; medium = 1 commodity; high = 2 commodities.
- **Construction sites** — you contribute a resource to help build a market. The number of workers needed depends on player count (2-3p: 2 of 4 spaces; 4p: 3 of 4; 5-6p: all 4). When the market completes, every contributor places an authority token.
- **Tunnels** — place your worker + pay a resource to advance the miner meeple. You gain a resource or an artifact card. At certain milestones, faction allegiance bonuses trigger.
- **Worker Activation Tank** — pay 3 Energy (lose 2 morale) or 3 Water (gain 2 morale) to gain a new worker die. You start with 2; your max is 4.
- **Artifact markets** — pay 3 different artifact types or 2 matching artifact cards to place 1 authority token.
- **Constructed market spaces** — use a built market's action (only if you have a star on it).

#### Option B: Retrieve Workers
Take **one or more** of your placed workers back into your personal supply. Then **roll all retrieved dice.**

**Knowledge check (threshold: 16):** After rolling, add up the knowledge values on ALL your **available** (off-board) worker dice + your position on the **knowledge track**. If the total hits **16 or higher**, your smartest worker deserts — you remove the die showing the **highest number**.

**Important:** Only your available (off-board) workers count toward this check. Workers still sitting on the board are safe.

**This is the core tension of Euphoria:** More workers means more actions per cycle. But more dice means higher knowledge totals and greater risk of desertion. You want to keep knowledge low and morale high — dumb and happy workers don't leave.

subtopics.title: Economy & Factions
subtopics.content:
#### Commodities vs. Resources
Your economy runs on two tiers, and understanding the difference is key to everything you do.

**Commodities** are what you generate directly by placing workers:
- **Water** (blue) — from Subterran territory
- **Energy** (yellow) — from Euphorian territory
- **Food** (brown) — from Wastelander territory
- **Bliss** (purple) — from Icarite territory

**Resources** are what you produce at markets by spending those commodities:
- **Gold** (yellow bars) — highest value
- **Stone** (gray blocks) — mid value
- **Clay** (red bricks) — most common

So the flow goes: place workers to earn commodities, spend commodities at resource markets to produce resources, then spend resources at construction sites to build markets and place authority tokens.

#### Faction Territories
The 3 ground factions (Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander) each have their own commodity areas, construction sites, and tunnel connections. The **Icarites** work differently — they have commodity areas and pre-built markets but no construction sites or tunnels. That makes them a unique faction to ally with.

#### Allegiance Track (4 Tiers)
Each faction has an allegiance track that advances as players interact with that faction's spaces. ALL players with active recruits of that faction benefit from tier bonuses:
- **Tier 1:** You gain +1 commodity at that faction's commodity area.
- **Tier 2:** Tunnel spaces grant you BOTH options (resource AND artifact card) instead of choosing one.
- **Tier 3:** All hidden (face-down) recruits of that faction **activate** for every player who has them.
- **Tier 4:** You place 1 authority star on each of your active recruits of that faction.

Tier 3 is huge — it flips hidden recruits for free. Tier 4 can place multiple authority tokens at once if you have 2 active recruits of that faction.

#### The Knowledge Track (1-6)
Your position on this track **adds to your knowledge check total** when you retrieve workers. The formula: available dice values + knowledge position. If that total hits 16, you lose a worker.
- **Lower is safer.** Every point of knowledge makes desertion more likely.
- You WANT low knowledge — smarter workers are the ones who escape.

#### The Morale Track (1-6)
Your morale value equals your **artifact card hand size**.
- **Higher is better.** More cards in hand means more flexibility for completing artifact sets.
- If your morale drops below your current hand size, you must discard down immediately.
- You WANT high morale — happy workers hold more resources for you.

**The mantra: keep your workers dumb and happy (low knowledge, high morale).**

#### Artifact Cards
Your artifact deck contains **6 types** (6 copies each, 36 total): Teddy Bear, Baseball Bat, Balloon, Bifocals, Book, and Board Game Box. You collect these from various board spaces and spend them as sets: pay **3 different types** or **2 matching** at an artifact market to place 1 authority star.

Those same artifact cards also fuel another option you should know about.

#### Ethical Dilemma Cards
You get 1 ethical dilemma card, and you can use it once per game. Pay 1 matching artifact or any 2 cards, then choose one of two paths:
- **Fight the Establishment** — draw 2 recruit cards, keep 1 face-down (gaining a new hidden recruit).
- **Contribute to Dystopia** — place 1 authority star directly on your dilemma card.

The right choice depends on your timing. Early game, a new hidden recruit can pay off big if its faction is close to Tier 3 allegiance. Late game, a direct authority star might be exactly what you need to close things out.

subtopics.title: Authority Tokens & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Ways to Place Authority Tokens
You have **10 authority tokens**. Place all 10 to win. Here's every path available to you:

1. **Build markets** — contribute resources to a construction site. When the market completes, every contributor places **1 authority token** on it. Anyone who did NOT contribute suffers that market's **ongoing penalty** (paying extra commodities, losing cards, restricted actions).

2. **Complete tunnels** — place your workers in tunnel spaces. When all spaces in a tunnel fill up, every player with a worker there places **1 authority token**. All workers then return to their owners.

3. **Artifact sets** — turn in a complete set of artifact cards at the appropriate board space to place **1 authority token**.

4. **Special board spaces** — certain action spaces let you place authority tokens directly for specific costs.

5. **Recruit abilities** — some recruit cards give you abilities that let you place tokens under specific conditions.

#### Market Penalties
When you help finish building a market, the tile flips to reveal an **ongoing penalty**. This penalty hits every player who did **not** contribute. Penalties include:
- Paying extra commodities for certain actions
- Losing a card when performing specific actions
- Being locked out of certain spaces

Contributors are exempt from the penalty AND get an authority token placed. That makes market construction doubly valuable — you gain a star and dodge a punishment.

#### Winning the Game
The game ends **immediately** when any player places their **10th authority token**. No final round, no waiting — instant victory the moment that last star lands.

#### If the Game Stalls
If no player can place their 10th token and progress becomes impossible, the player with the **fewest remaining authority tokens** wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities (Turns 1-5)
- **Generate commodities first.** You need commodities to do anything else. Place your 2 starting workers on commodity generation spaces to stockpile water, energy, food, or bliss.
- **Focus on 1-2 faction territories.** Spreading across all 4 factions dilutes your progress. Pick the factions that match your face-up recruit's allegiance and concentrate there.
- **Get a 3rd worker early.** Going from 2 to 3 workers gives you a 50% efficiency boost — you get 3 placements per retrieve cycle instead of 2. Visit the Worker Activation Tank by turn 5-7.
- **Contribute to markets.** Placing an authority token via market construction is efficient AND protects you from the market's penalty. That's two-for-one value.

#### Managing the Knowledge Check (Threshold: 16)
Remember the formula: available worker dice values + knowledge track position. If the total hits 16, your smartest worker deserts.
- **Keep your knowledge track position LOW.** A lower position gives you more headroom for dice values.
- **Retrieve fewer workers when possible.** Only off-board workers count toward the check. Leaving one on a commodity area reduces your total.
- **Use bumping aggressively.** Bumping an opponent forces them to roll and check — potentially costing them a worker while you take the space's benefit for yourself.
- **Watch for doubles.** If 2+ of your available workers show the same number, you can place them all in one turn — a massive tempo swing.

#### Worker Activation: When to Get Worker #3
- Pay **3 Energy** (lose 2 morale) or **3 Water** (gain 2 morale) at the Worker Activation Tank.
- The water path is strictly better — you gain morale instead of losing it. Prioritize water whenever you can.
- A 3rd worker gives you a 50% efficiency boost. Aim to get it by turn 5-7.
- Don't rush to 4 workers. That raises your knowledge check total significantly and risks constant desertions. Stabilize at 3 first.

#### Authority Token Priorities
1. **Markets** — best value. You place a token AND avoid a penalty. Always contribute if you can afford the resource.
2. **Tunnels** — good value but you need coordination. Other players' workers have to be in the same tunnel.
3. **Artifact sets** — reliable but slow. You need to draw and hold specific card types.
4. **Special spaces** — situational. Best for closing out the game when you need your last 1-2 tokens.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Ignoring market construction.** Non-contributors get penalized AND miss an authority token. That's a double punishment for sitting out.
- **Rushing to 4 workers.** With high knowledge, you'll lose workers to desertion constantly. Stabilize at 3 before even considering a 4th.
- **Hoarding artifact cards.** Cards in hand only matter if you complete sets. If your morale drops and your hand size shrinks, you discard them anyway. Spend your sets or trade extras.
- **Neglecting morale.** Low morale = tiny hand size = can't hold artifact sets = you lose a key path to authority tokens.
- **Not watching opponents' authority tokens.** Count how many stars each player has placed. If someone has 8-9 down, they're about to win — block their likeliest path or race to finish first.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Euphoria

**After Setup**: Look down at what you have in front of you — 2 worker dice (rolled, numbers visible), 10 authority tokens, markers on the knowledge and morale tracks, 1 face-up recruit, 1 face-down recruit, and some artifact cards. Your goal: place all 10 authority tokens before anyone else.

**Turn 1 — Place a Worker**: Start by looking at your face-up recruit's faction. Place a worker on a commodity generation space in that faction's territory — you'll gain water, energy, food, or bliss depending on which faction it is. Now you have something to work with.

**Turn 2 — Place Your Other Worker**: Put your second worker on another commodity space, or if you already have commodities, try a resource market to convert them into gold, stone, or clay. You're stockpiling materials for the mid-game.

**Turn 3 — Retrieve Workers**: With both workers out on the board, you have nothing left to place — so now you retrieve. Take both workers back and roll them. Here comes your first knowledge check: add both dice values + your knowledge track position. If the total stays under 16, you're safe. For example, say you roll a 3 and a 5 with your knowledge at 4 — that's 12, well under the threshold.

**Turns 4-6 — Build Your Engine**: Now you're in a rhythm. Generate more commodities. Visit a resource market to produce stone or clay. Then contribute a resource to a market construction site — when that market finishes, you place an authority token. That's 1 of your 10 done.

**Mid-Game — Get a 3rd Worker**: Once you've built up some reserves, head to the Worker Activation Tank. Pay 3 Water (gain 2 morale — the better option) or 3 Energy (lose 2 morale) to gain a 3rd worker die. Roll it and add it to your supply. Now you get 3 placements per cycle instead of 2, which is a big jump in efficiency. But be careful — 3 dice means higher knowledge check totals when you retrieve.

**Mid-to-Late Game — Multiple Paths**: With your engine running, you want to spread across multiple scoring paths. Keep contributing to markets for authority tokens and penalty immunity. Place workers in tunnels that are close to completion. Collect artifact card sets and turn them in when you have a match. Work toward your face-down recruit's faction allegiance to unlock its special ability — hitting Tier 3 could flip it for free.

**Endgame — Race to 10**: Once you've placed 7-8 authority tokens, every single turn matters. Scan the board for your fastest remaining path — can you complete a tunnel? Do you have an artifact set ready to turn in? Is a market about to finish? Find your quickest route to 10 and go for it.

**Victory**: The moment you place your 10th authority token, you win immediately. No waiting for the round to finish — the game stops right there.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Knowledge Management
The knowledge check threshold is 16 (available dice values + knowledge track position). Playing well means staying just below that line while maximizing your worker count.

- **Sweet spot: knowledge track at 3-4** with 3 workers. That gives you room for dice totals up to 12-13 without desertion. At knowledge 5+ with 3 workers, even average rolls trigger desertions.
- **Bump opponents strategically.** Every bump forces them to roll and check. If you suspect their knowledge total is borderline, bumping can cost them a worker — and you still get the space's benefit.
- **Selective retrieval matters.** You don't have to retrieve ALL your workers — just one or more. Leaving a high-knowledge die on the board keeps it out of your check total.

#### Morale Optimization
Your morale equals your artifact card hand size. At morale 1-2, you can barely hold a single set. At morale 5-6, you hold enough to complete sets flexibly.

- **Water path for Worker Activation** (+2 morale) is always preferred over the Energy path (-2 morale). Plan your commodity generation accordingly.
- **Ethical Dilemma timing:** "Fight the Establishment" gives you a new hidden recruit — powerful if a Tier 3 allegiance is close to triggering. "Contribute to Dystopia" places an authority star directly — better when you're in the endgame race and need every star you can get.

#### Faction-Specific Tactics
- **Euphorians** have the strongest artifact generation pipeline. Pair Euphorian recruits for chained artifact effects that accelerate set completion.
- **Wastelanders** dominate resource production. Two Wastelander recruits can fuel rapid market construction, letting you lock down stars while penalizing everyone else.
- **Subterrans** control the tunnel networks. Tunnel completion places authority tokens AND returns your workers — that's strong tempo. Subterran recruits amplify tunnel bonuses further.
- **Icarites** excel at track manipulation — pushing your knowledge down and morale up. Their pre-built markets skip the construction step entirely, giving you access to market actions without spending resources to build.

#### Authority Token Acceleration
- **Markets are the best value in the game.** Each market you help build gives you a star AND inflicts a penalty on non-contributors. In a 5-6 player game, being shut out of 2-3 markets creates devastating cumulative penalties.
- **Time market completion deliberately.** If you can finish a market on a turn when opponents are low on resources, they can't contribute — maximizing the penalty spread.
- **Tunnel coordination:** Tunnel completion requires all spaces filled. Place a worker early to "reserve" your spot, then wait for others to fill in. If nobody joins, retrieve and redeploy elsewhere — don't let your worker sit idle.
- **Allegiance Tier 4** places authority tokens on ALL your active recruits of that faction. With 2 active recruits (your original + a flipped hidden one), that's 2 stars from a single tier trigger. Build toward this with paired-faction recruits.

#### Doubles as a Weapon
With 3+ available dice, your odds of rolling doubles are significant. Doubles let you place 2 workers on one turn — effectively doubling your output for that cycle.

- **Retrieve all your workers at once** to maximize your chance of rolling doubles.
- **Use doubles offensively:** place one worker to bump an opponent off a space, then use your second placement on the now-cleared space or an adjacent high-value one.
- Don't plan around doubles, but always check for them at the start of your turn.
